title: Fix MongoDB Projection to Ignore Embedded Fields with Optional Keys

statement.markdown: Write a MongoDB projection function that ignores embedded fields with optional keys. Given a MongoDB collection document, it should project only the required fields and ignore the optional ones.
statement.inputFormat: A MongoDB collection document as a JSON string
statement.outputFormat: The projected document as a JSON string
statement.notes: The function should handle nested fields and ignore embedded fields with optional keys.

constraints.description: The number of documents in the collection

examples.explanation: The function ignores the optional key "street" in the address field
The function ignores both optional keys "state" and "country" in the address field

hints.text: Use the MongoDB $project operator to project only the required fields

editorial.title: Editorial: MongoDB Query Projection
editorial.intuition: The correct approach is to use the MongoDB $project operator to project only the required fields and ignore the optional ones.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute-force approach would be to manually check each field and decide whether to include it or not, but this would be inefficient and prone to errors.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the $project operator with the $ifNull expression to project only the required fields and ignore the optional ones.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The $project operator is designed to project only the specified fields, and the $ifNull expression is used to handle null values.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: Input: {"name": "John", "address": {"street": "123 Main St", "city": "Anytown"}}; Output: {"name": "John", "address": {"city": "Anytown"}}
commonMistakes: Including optional fields in the projection
